channel.title: Delicious posts Feed - Pinterest
channel.description: Fresh posts and cooking inspiration for Pinterest discovery

item.title: Classic Banana Bread with a Moist, Tender Crumb and a Hint of Cinnamon
item.description: This banana bread is a cozy classic, perfect for using up ripe bananas. The touch of cinnamon adds warmth, and it’s wonderfully moist thanks to a simple trick with the bananas. Great for breakfast or an afternoon snack! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 60min | Serves: 8 | #7 #Easypost #post #cooking #foodie
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 60 minutes

⏰ Total Time: 75 minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 cups ripe mashed bananas (about 3 medium bananas)
  • 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted and cooled
  • 3/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 2 large eggs, room temperature
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. 1 Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan or line it with parchment paper. This step is crucial because a properly prepared pan ensures your banana bread won’t stick and will come out cleanly. Take your time to grease all the corners well, or else you might struggle to remove the loaf intact later. Also, preheating the oven fully before baking helps the bread rise evenly and develop a nice crust. 10 minutes Oven fully preheated to 350°F and loaf pan well greased
  2. 2 In a large bowl, mash the ripe bananas thoroughly with a fork until mostly smooth with just a few small lumps remaining. The ripeness of the bananas is key here; the riper they are, the sweeter and more flavorful your bread will be. Mashing well releases natural sugars and moisture, which keeps the bread tender. Don’t rush this step—taking a minute to get the right texture makes a big difference in the final crumb. 5 minutes Banana mash is smooth with small lumps, no large chunks
  3. 3 Add the melted butter to the mashed bananas and stir to combine. The melted butter coats the flour later and helps create a moist texture. Using melted rather than softened butter avoids overmixing and keeps the bread denser and more tender. Mix gently to avoid deflating the batter, which can happen if you beat it too vigorously. 2 minutes Butter fully incorporated, batter smooth and glossy
  4. 4 Stir in the sugar, eggs, and vanilla extract until just combined. The sugar sweetens the bread while the eggs provide structure and richness. Be careful not to overmix here either; just stir until you don’t see separate streaks of egg or sugar. Overmixing develops gluten in the flour, which can make the bread tough instead of tender. 3 minutes Batter uniform in color and texture, no visible egg streaks
  5. 5 In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon. This ensures the leavening agent and spices are evenly distributed throughout the flour, preventing clumps or uneven rising. Baking soda reacts with the acidity in the bananas to help the bread rise, so even mixing is important for a consistent crumb. 3 minutes Dry ingredients well combined, no lumps
  6. 6 Gently fold the dry ingredients into the wet mixture using a spatula. Do this carefully to avoid overmixing; you want to combine until just no flour streaks remain. Overmixing activates gluten and can make the bread dense and chewy rather than soft and tender. The batter should be thick but spreadable. 3 minutes Batter uniform with no visible flour, thick but smooth
  7. 7 Pour the batter into your prepared loaf pan and smooth the top with a spatula. This helps the bread bake evenly and look nice when sliced. You can gently tap the pan on the counter to release any large air bubbles trapped in the batter, which could cause holes or uneven texture in the finished loaf. 2 minutes Batter evenly spread in pan, surface smooth
  8. 8 Bake in the preheated oven for about 55-65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean or with just a few moist crumbs. Avoid opening the oven door too early or too often, as this can cause the bread to collapse. If the top browns too quickly, loosely tent with foil halfway through baking. 55-65 minutes Golden brown crust and clean toothpick test
  9. 9 Let the banana bread cool in the pan for 10-15 minutes before transferring to a wire rack. Cooling in the pan helps it set so it doesn’t fall apart when you remove it. Once on the rack, cooling completely prevents sogginess and helps the flavors settle. Slice only when fully cooled for clean, neat pieces. 20 minutes Loaf firm to the touch and cool enough to slice

📌 Pinterest Tags: #7 #Easypost #post #cooking #foodie #homemade #delicious #mashed #unsalted #granulated

📌 Save this post to Pinterest!

Perfect for meal planning and sharing with friends

media:content.media:description: This banana bread is a cozy classic, perfect for using up ripe bananas. The touch of cinnamon adds warmth, and it’s wonderfully moist thanks to a simple trick with the bananas. Great for breakfast or an afternoon snack!
